summary: Vivid audience recording of a fiery second‑set, highlighted by Jerry’s soaring guitar and standout renditions of Jack‑a‑Roe, World to Give and Playing in the Band.
review: The set bursts out of the gate with a jam that rolls straight into drums before launching into a spectacular sequence of Jack‑a‑Roe, Playing in the Band > Shakedown Street > World to Give. Reviewers rave about Jerry Garcia’s “big fat chords” and lemon‑squeezer arpeggios, while noting the recording captures lively audience ambience despite an out‑of‑phase stereo issue.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong musical performance (standout jams and guitar work) weighted heavily, offset slightly by a modest recording flaw (phase problem). Consistent high star ratings across four reviews give confidence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Lively, personable sound with clear audience presence; however both channels are out of phase, causing a slightly smeared stereo image.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second‑set flows organically from a drum‑focused jam into a seamless medley, keeping momentum high and audience energy up throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia is described as having "it" that night—big fat chords, lemon‑squeezer arpeggios, and a magical tone.
band_member_comments.Drums: The opening jam “into drums” is highlighted as a driving force for the set.
